tagline_en: How purified sand becomes the most precisely manufactured object humans make — and why yield math and $20B fabs explain half of the AI supply chain.
tagline_zh: 看懂一把沙子如何变成人类制造精度之巅的物件——并理解良率数学与两百亿美元一座的晶圆厂,如何解释 AI 供应链的半壁江山。

sections.heading_en: Step Zero: From Beach Sand to a Perfect Crystal
sections.heading_zh: 第零步:从沙子到完美单晶
sections.body_en:
Every chip begins as quartz — silicon dioxide, the main mineral in ordinary sand. Smelting quartz with carbon in an arc furnace yields 'metallurgical-grade' silicon, roughly 98–99% pure. That sounds clean; for electronics it is filth. A transistor works by deliberately adding a tiny number of impurity atoms ('dopants') to silicon to control exactly how it conducts. The trick only works if the background material is almost perfectly empty: a stray phosphorus or boron atom in the wrong place is indistinguishable from an intentional one. So raw silicon is converted into a gas (trichlorosilane), distilled like whiskey to strip out contaminants, then re-deposited as hyperpure polysilicon. Wacker, one of the leading producers, ships semiconductor-grade polysilicon at better than 99.9999999999% purity — critical impurities measured in parts per trillion.

Purity alone is not enough; the atoms must also line up. Polysilicon is a jumble of small crystals, and the boundaries between grains scatter electrons, making every transistor behave slightly differently. The fix is the Czochralski process: melt the polysilicon at about 1,414°C, dip a small seed crystal into the melt, and pull it upward slowly while rotating. Atoms freeze onto the seed in perfect crystalline order, growing a single flawless crystal — a mirror-gray ingot up to 300mm in diameter and a couple of meters long. Diamond wire saws then slice the ingot into discs under a millimeter thick, which are ground and polished until the surface is flat to within atoms. That 300mm mirror is the canvas. Every GPU and every HBM stack in this series is drawn on one.
sections.body_zh:
每一颗芯片的起点都是石英——普通沙子里的二氧化硅。石英与碳在电弧炉里冶炼,得到纯度约 98–99% 的「冶金级硅」。听起来很纯,但对电子工业来说这是废料:晶体管的工作原理,恰恰是人为地往硅里掺入极少量「杂质原子」(掺杂),以此精确控制导电行为。这套把戏成立的前提,是本底材料干净到近乎绝对——一颗跑错位置的磷原子或硼原子,和你有意掺进去的那颗没有任何区别。所以原料硅要先转化成气体(三氯氢硅),像蒸馏威士忌一样反复精馏去杂,再还原沉积成「超纯多晶硅」。龙头厂商之一的瓦克(Wacker),出厂的半导体级多晶硅纯度超过 99.9999999999%,关键杂质以万亿分之一(ppt)计。

但光纯还不够,原子还得排整齐。多晶硅是无数小晶粒的杂拌,晶粒之间的晶界会散射电子,让每只晶体管的脾气都略有不同。解决办法是直拉法(Czochralski):把多晶硅在约 1414°C 熔化,浸入一粒籽晶,边旋转边缓缓上提,原子沿着籽晶的晶格逐层凝固,长成一根直径 300 毫米、长约两米、通体无缺陷的单晶硅锭。随后用金刚石线锯把硅锭切成不到 1 毫米厚的圆片,再研磨、抛光到原子级平整——一面完美的镜子。这张 300 毫米的「画布」,就是整个系列所有故事的起点:每一颗 GPU、每一叠 HBM,都画在它上面。

sections.heading_en: The Fab Loop: Building a Skyscraper with Light and Stencils
sections.heading_zh: 晶圆厂循环:用光和模板盖摩天大楼
sections.body_en:
A finished chip is not a flat drawing but a three-dimensional city: transistors occupy the ground floor, with fifteen or more levels of copper wiring stacked above like highway interchanges. The fab builds it one layer at a time, looping through five basic verbs:

Deposit — lay an ultra-thin, uniform film (insulator, metal, or silicon) over the whole wafer; the finest films are grown one atomic layer at a time (ALD).
Pattern — coat the wafer with light-sensitive photoresist and project this layer's design through a photomask, a quartz stencil, shrinking the image 4x onto the wafer. The most advanced machines (EUV) do this with 13.5nm-wavelength light.
Etch — use plasma or chemicals to eat away material wherever the developed resist leaves it exposed, transferring the pattern from resist into film.
Implant — fire dopant ions into selected regions to create the electrically active hearts of the transistors, then anneal at high temperature to heal the crystal.
Polish (CMP) — grind the surface flat with a chemical slurry so the next layer starts on a plane.

Then repeat. A leading-edge process uses more than 60 lithography layers, and each carries its own cluster of deposition, etch, clean, and metrology steps; Intel has shown a single metal-wiring scheme that alone required three EUV exposures and over 40 process steps. In total a wafer traverses on the order of a thousand steps over roughly three months of around-the-clock processing — and an error at step 700 scraps the previous 699. What is genuinely hard to copy about a fab is not any single machine; it is the proven recipe for running a thousand steps in a row without failing.
sections.body_zh:
成品芯片不是一张平面图,而是一座立体城市:晶体管住在「一楼」,上面再架十五层以上的铜线,像层层叠叠的高架桥。晶圆厂的做法是一层一层往上盖,反复循环五个基本动作:

沉积——在整片晶圆上铺一层极薄且均匀的膜(绝缘体、金属或硅),最精细的膜要用原子层沉积(ALD)一层原子一层原子地长。
光刻——先涂上感光的光刻胶,再把这一层的电路图案透过光掩模(一块石英「模板」)用光投影到晶圆上,同时缩小 4 倍;最先进的 EUV 光刻机用的是 13.5 纳米波长的极紫外光。
刻蚀——用等离子体或化学品,把显影后裸露之处的材料啃掉,把图案从胶转移进膜里。
离子注入——把掺杂离子加速打进指定区域,造出晶体管的电学心脏,再高温退火修复晶格。
化学机械抛光(CMP)——用磨浆把表面磨平,让下一层在平面上开工。

然后循环往复。先进制程要用超过 60 个光刻层,每层还带着自己的一串沉积、刻蚀、清洗、量测工序;英特尔展示过,仅一套金属布线方案就需要 3 次 EUV 曝光、共 40 多道工序。加总下来,一片晶圆要走上千道工序、日夜连转约三个月——第 700 步出错,前 699 步全部报废。晶圆厂真正难以复制的,从来不是某一台机器,而是把上千道工序连续跑通而不出错的那份「配方」。

sections.heading_en: What a 'Node' Actually Is — and Why '3nm' Measures Nothing
sections.heading_zh: 「制程节点」到底是什么——以及为什么「3 纳米」并不是尺寸
sections.body_en:
'3nm' sounds like a measurement. It isn't. A process node is a complete, frozen, qualified recipe — the full combination of masks, materials, machine settings, and design rules that a fab sells capacity on. Nothing on a '3nm' chip is 3nm wide; the tightest metal lines sit 20–30nm apart. The numbers that actually mean something are pitches (how closely gates and wires can be packed), the transistor density they produce, and PPA — performance, power, area. Each node also spawns a family of variants (N3B, N3E, N3P, N3X…) tuned toward cost, phones, or high-performance computing.

Nodes matter economically because for fifty years each new one made transistors cheaper — that was the commercial engine of Moore's Law. The engine is now sputtering. SemiAnalysis's cost model of TSMC's 3nm found cost per transistor improving only ~15% versus 5nm in the best case — the weakest scaling of any major node in more than fifty years — while a single 3nm photomask set costs about $40M, versus over $10M at 7nm and around $1M at 28nm. Design costs balloon alongside. Three consequences follow: fewer and fewer companies can afford leading-edge designs at all; value migrates toward chiplets and advanced packaging, which squeeze more out of existing transistors (Module 04 territory); and node names have become marketing weapons in the TSMC–Intel–Samsung race, where the honest scorecard is density, power, and cost — not the number in the brand (Sector Brief 02).
sections.body_zh:
很多人以为「3 纳米」是某个物理尺寸。不是。制程节点是一份完整的、被冻结并通过验证的「菜谱」:全套掩模、材料、机台参数与设计规则的组合,晶圆厂以它为单位对外卖产能。「3nm」芯片上没有任何东西宽 3 纳米——最密的金属线间距也在 20–30 纳米量级。真正有意义的是「间距」(栅极与导线能排多密)、由此决定的晶体管密度,以及 PPA(性能/功耗/面积)。每代节点还会衍生一族变体(N3B、N3E、N3P、N3X……),分别向成本、手机或高性能计算倾斜。

节点之所以在经济上举足轻重,是因为过去五十年里,每一代新节点都让晶体管变得更便宜——这正是摩尔定律的商业内核。如今这台发动机正在熄火:SemiAnalysis 对台积电 3nm 的成本模型显示,N3 的每晶体管成本最多只比 N5 改善约 15%,是 50 多年来主要节点中最弱的一次微缩;而一套 3nm 光掩模就要约 4000 万美元(7nm 超 1000 万美元,28nm 约 100 万美元),设计成本也随之暴涨。三个后果:玩得起先进制程的公司越来越少;价值向 chiplet 与先进封装迁移——用封装从既有晶体管里榨出更多性能(第 04 篇的主场);「纳米数」彻底沦为台积电-英特尔-三星竞赛中的营销武器,真正的记分牌是密度、功耗与成本,而不是品牌名里的数字(第 02 篇)。

sections.heading_en: Yield: The Exponential That Rules Chip Economics
sections.heading_zh: 良率:统治芯片经济学的那条指数曲线
sections.body_en:
Picture the wafer as a sheet of postage stamps; each stamp is one die, the rectangle that becomes a chip. Defects — a particle that landed mid-process, an etch a nanometer too deep — scatter across the wafer roughly at random, with an average defect density called D0 (defects per cm²). One killer defect usually means one dead die. The math is merciless: die yield ≈ e^(−area × D0), exponential in die area. Small dies slip between the defects; big dies are targets. Double the area and your survival probability squares: if a small chip yields 90% on a given line, a chip four times its size yields about 66% at the same D0. AI GPUs live at the extreme — nearly all are designed against the ~858mm² 'reticle limit,' the largest area one exposure can print — so early in a node's life their yields can sit below half.

Three consequences. First, yield learning is the business: a node launches with high D0 that falls for years, which is why margins on a new node start ugly and improve — and why TSMC's aggressive N3B struggled while the relaxed N3E that replaced it showed, in TSMC's words, consistently healthier defect density. Second, giant dies pay a superlinear area tax — a core reason AI silicon is moving to chiplets: split one huge die into several small ones that each yield well, then reassemble them inside the package. Third, salvage: designs carry spare cores and memory so a partially defective die can be sold, bad blocks disabled, as a cheaper SKU.
sections.body_zh:
把晶圆想象成一整版邮票,每张邮票是一颗「裸片」(die)——将来会变成芯片的那个小矩形。缺陷——制程中途落下的一粒尘埃、深了一纳米的刻蚀——大致随机地撒在整片晶圆上,平均密度记作 D0(每平方厘米缺陷数)。一颗裸片只要中了一个致命缺陷,通常就报废。数学非常残酷:裸片良率 ≈ e^(−面积×D0),对面积呈指数关系。小芯片容易在缺陷之间「漏网」,大芯片则是活靶子:面积翻倍,存活概率取平方。假设某条产线上小芯片良率 90%,同样的 D0 下,4 倍大的芯片良率只有约 66%。AI GPU 活在最极端处——几乎全部顶着约 858 平方毫米的「光罩极限」(单次曝光能印出的最大面积)设计,所以新节点初期,它们的良率常常不到一半。

三个推论。第一,良率学习本身就是这门生意:节点上市时 D0 很高,随后数年持续下降,这就是新节点毛利率总是先难看、后改善的原因——台积电激进的 N3B 良率挣扎,接棒的、放松了设计规则的 N3E 才做到官方口径的「缺陷密度持续优于上一代」,是教科书级案例。第二,巨型裸片要交超线性的「面积税」,这是 AI 芯片转向 chiplet 的根本动因之一:把一颗巨片拆成几颗小片,各自良率高,再在封装里拼回去。第三,「屏蔽降级销售」:设计里预留冗余核心与存储,部分缺陷的裸片关掉坏块,照样当低配 SKU 卖钱。

sections.heading_zh: 洁净室与两百亿美元的门票
sections.body_en:
Because one invisible particle can kill a die, the fab must be among the cleanest places humans have ever built. Cleanroom air is scrubbed by wall-to-wall filtration until it holds orders of magnitude fewer particles than a hospital operating room; wafers travel in sealed pods (FOUPs) carried by overhead robots; people — the dirtiest objects in the building — wear full-body 'bunny suits' and touch nothing.

Yet the cleanroom shell is the cheap part. The money is in the tools that fill it. A single EUV lithography machine sells for about $225M and can pattern more than $650M worth of finished wafers per year; lithography alone consumes ~35% of the cost of a 3nm process. Fill a building with hundreds of such tools and, by Intel's own arithmetic, a leading-edge fab costs $25–30B per 10,000 wafers per week of capacity — TSMC has quoted roughly $42B for its Arizona 3nm capacity. TSMC's total planned US investment — three additional fabs, two advanced packaging plants, and an R&D center on top of the original $65B three-fab Phoenix site — comes to $165B.

The economics follow directly. A fab's cost is mostly depreciation, which burns whether or not wafers move, so fabs must run flat-out around the clock; swings in utilization pass almost one-for-one into profit — that is foundry cyclicality. And each generation's fab costs more than the last, so only firms with locked-in demand for the next node can fund the next fab. That flywheel built the most concentrated critical industry on earth — the subject of Sector Brief 02.
sections.body_zh:
既然一粒看不见的尘埃就能杀死一颗裸片,晶圆厂本身就必须是人类建过的最干净的地方之一。洁净室的空气经过铺满顶棚的过滤系统持续置换,颗粒物比医院手术室还要少几个数量级;晶圆装在密封盒(FOUP)里由天轨机器人运送;人——全楼最脏的污染源——裹进连体「兔子服」,全程不碰任何东西。

但洁净室的壳只是小头,大头是塞进去的机器。一台 EUV 光刻机售价约 2.25 亿美元,一年却能曝光出价值超过 6.5 亿美元的成品晶圆;仅光刻一项,就占 3nm 制程成本的约 35%。把几百台这样的机器装满一栋楼——按英特尔自己的算术,一座先进晶圆厂每「万片/周」产能需要 250–300 亿美元资本开支;台积电给亚利桑那 3nm 产能报的数更是约 420 亿美元。台积电在美国的总盘子——在凤凰城原有 650 亿美元、三座厂的基础上,再加三座新厂、两座先进封装厂和一个研发中心——合计 1650 亿美元。

经济学随资本结构而来:晶圆厂的成本大头是折旧,机器开不开工都在烧钱,所以厂必须昼夜满负荷运转;稼动率的波动几乎一比一传导为利润波动——这就是代工的周期性。而且每一代厂都比上一代更贵,只有锁定了下一代需求的公司,才建得起下一座厂。正是这个飞轮,造就了地球上集中度最高的关键产业——第 02 篇的主题。

images.caption_en: A monocrystalline silicon ingot — one flawless crystal grown from hyperpure polysilicon, ready to be sliced into wafers.
images.caption_zh: 单晶硅锭——由超纯多晶硅生长出的一整根无缺陷单晶,等待被切成晶圆。

images.caption_en: Silicon wafers from 2 to 8 inches in diameter. Bigger discs mean more chips per pass; leading-edge fabs today run 300mm (12-inch) wafers.
images.caption_zh: 直径 2 到 8 英寸的硅晶圆。盘子越大,一次加工出的芯片越多;当今先进晶圆厂使用 300 毫米(12 英寸)晶圆。

images.caption_en: Inside a semiconductor cleanroom: filtered air, full-body suits, and nothing touched by hand — because a single particle can kill a die.
images.caption_zh: 半导体洁净室内部:全过滤空气、连体洁净服、任何东西不徒手触碰——因为一粒尘埃就能杀死一颗裸片。

own_fig.concept_en: The sand-to-silicon pipeline and the fab loop
own_fig.concept_zh: 从沙子到硅片的流水线与晶圆厂循环
own_fig.spec: Two-panel box-and-arrow diagram, no axes. PANEL A (top, left-to-right linear flow, 6 boxes with small icons): [Quartz sand (SiO2)] → [Metallurgical silicon, 98–99% pure, arc furnace] → [Gas distillation (trichlorosilane), Siemens process] → [Hyperpure polysilicon, >99.9999999999% (source: Wacker press release 2025-07-17)] → [Czochralski pull at ~1,414°C → 300mm single-crystal ingot] → [Wire-saw + polish → mirror-flat blank wafer]. PANEL B (bottom): a circular loop of 5 stations labeled Deposit → Pattern (litho: resist + photomask + EUV 13.5nm light, image shrunk 4x) → Etch → Implant + anneal → CMP polish, with a bold annotation on the loop: 'repeat for >60 lithography layers (source: SemiAnalysis, The Dark Side of the Semiconductor Design Renaissance) ≈ on the order of 1,000 steps, ~3 months per wafer'. Loop exit arrow → [Patterned wafer with die grid] → [Test → dice → package]. Three cost callout tags pinned to Panel B: 'EUV tool $225M, prints >$650M wafers/yr (SemiAnalysis, How to Kill 2 Monopolies with 1 Tool)'; 'litho ≈35% of 3nm process cost (SemiAnalysis, Sculpta article)'; '3nm mask set ≈$40M (SemiAnalysis, Dark Side article)'. Color code: purification steps in sand/amber, crystal/wafer steps in gray-blue, loop stations in teal, cost tags in red.

why_en: This module is the foundation under every sector brief. Equipment capex cycles (05) are a function of node transitions; the gross margin and ramp timing of each new accelerator (01) is set by the yield learning curve; advanced packaging (04) exists because of the exponential area tax on big dies; foundry cyclicality and moats (02) follow from a depreciation-dominated cost structure and a flywheel where only locked-in demand can fund the next fab; and the difficulty of subsidized fab relocation (10) is the difficulty of moving cleanrooms, hyperpure supply chains, and thousand-step recipes. When a company reports 'defect density on track' or 'N2 ramping,' you can now translate that into margins and capacity.
why_zh: 这一篇是读懂全系列的地基。设备篇(05)的资本开支周期,本质上是节点迁移的函数;加速器篇(01)里每颗新芯片的毛利率与爬产节奏,由良率学习曲线决定;封装篇(04)的存在理由,就是大裸片那笔指数级的「面积税」;代工篇(02)的周期性与护城河,来自折旧主导的成本结构,以及「只有锁定需求者才建得起下一座厂」的飞轮;地缘篇(10)里补贴迁厂之难,难在洁净室、超纯材料链和上千道工序的「配方」无法整体搬运。以后再看到「缺陷密度符合预期」「N2 开始爬产」这类新闻,你可以直接把它翻译成毛利率与产能的变化。
